understanding.current_goal: Trace Zenith CSS determinism contract to implementation
understanding.latest_decision: CSS blocks are ordered by dependency depth via compiler sort
understanding.next_action: Obtain zenith_compiler crate source (packages/compiler/src/script.rs and compiler.rs) to verify whether compile_structured_with_source normalizes module_id to forward slashes and enforces duplicate (module_id, order) uniqueness

findings.claim: Module IDs passed to the compiler are not forward-slash normalized at the bundler boundary
findings.location: packages/bundler/src/plugin/zenith_loader.rs — resolve_id hook (canonicalize) and compile_zen_source (passes id unmodified to compile_structured_with_source)
findings.notes: resolve_id uses std::fs::canonicalize which returns OS-native separators (backslashes on Windows). compile_zen_source passes this id directly to compile_structured_with_source without calling utils::clean_path or any normalization. If the compiler uses this id as module_id in ExtractedStyleBlock, per-block hashes will differ cross-OS, violating DETERMINISM.md §2.1. Cannot confirm whether the compiler normalizes internally — compiler source not in repository evidence. LF normalization IS confirmed at this boundary: compile_zen_source does source.replace("\r\n", "\n") before compilation.

patch.next: Obtain zenith_compiler crate source (packages/compiler/src/script.rs and packages/compiler/src/compiler.rs) to verify whether compile_structured_with_source normalizes the id parameter to forward slashes for module_id, and whether it enforces duplicate (module_id, order) uniqueness before returning CompilerOutput.style_blocks

update.reason: Compiler source files are not in repository evidence, so the next step requires expanding the evidence set to include the compiler crate. The bundler boundary analysis is complete: LF normalization is confirmed, module ID canonicalization is NOT enforced at the bundler-to-compiler boundary, and duplicate (module_id, order) enforcement cannot be verified without compiler source.

stopping_point: Stopped after tracing the compiler input boundary in zenith_loader.rs. Confirmed LF normalization is applied before compile_structured_with_source, but could not verify module ID canonicalization or duplicate (module_id, order) enforcement inside the compiler because zenith_compiler source files are not listed in repository evidence.
completed: Verified LF normalization at compiler input boundary: compile_zen_source applies source.replace("\r\n", "\n") before calling compile_structured_with_source

Identified that resolve_id uses std::fs::canonicalize (OS-specific separators) and compile_zen_source passes id to compiler without forward-slash normalization — module ID canonicalization is not enforced at the bundler-to-compiler boundary

Confirmed compiler source files (zenith_compiler::script, zenith_compiler::compiler) are not available in repository evidence, blocking further verification of internal compiler normalization and duplicate identity enforcement
